title: Dahi Aloo

ingredients.title: Slurry (to prevent curdling)
items: Besan (gram flour) – 1 tsp mixed into 2 tbsp water

ingredients.title: Finishing & Garnish
items: A drizzle of ghee for aroma
Optional: fried baby potatoes for extra crisp texture

instruction.title: Step 1 — Prep the Potatoes
steps: Wash and boil baby potatoes until 90% cooked.
Peel and lightly prick each potato with a fork.
Heat 1 tbsp ghee in a pan and shallow-fry until golden & crisp.
This step gives restaurant-style texture.

instruction.title: Step 2 — Flavor Base
steps: Heat remaining ghee in a heavy pan.
Add cumin seeds → allow them to crackle.
Add hing and ginger paste → sauté until aromatic.
Mix in green chilies.

instruction.title: Step 3 — Build the Gravy
steps: Lower heat. Add turmeric + chili powder + coriander powder → quick stir.
Add the besan slurry → cook for 1 minute.
Keep flame low and add whisked yogurt gradually while stirring continuously.
Prevents curdling — smooth and creamy texture.
Add water to adjust consistency (medium-thick, smooth gravy).
Add salt and bring to a gentle simmer for 5–7 minutes.

instruction.title: Step 4 — Final Assembly
steps: Add fried baby potatoes and coat evenly.
Add roasted fennel powder + garam masala + kasuri methi.
Simmer covered for 5 minutes so potatoes absorb flavors.

instruction.title: Finishing Touch (Restaurant Secret)
steps: Turn off heat → rest covered for 5 minutes.
Drizzle hot ghee on the top.
Garnish with fresh coriander.

instruction.title: Serving Suggestions
steps: Pairs perfectly with:
Butter naan / Garlic naan
Tandoori roti
Jeera rice / Steamed basmati
Laccha paratha

chefTips: Keep flame low while adding yogurt.
Use baby potatoes for the best presentation.
Add a pinch of sugar if yogurt is too sour — balances flavors.
Freshly roasted fennel and kasuri methi elevate aroma.
